title: Purged Forward CV With Embargo For Feature Acceptance
summary: Public time-series validation references show standard shuffled/grouped CV can stay optimistic when labels depend on near-future events.
rationale: Public time-series validation references show standard shuffled/grouped CV can stay optimistic when labels depend on near-future events. Here the target is explicitly next-lap, so neighboring observations inside the same race are highly dependent. A stronger validation scheme may reject misleading features and select more leaderboard-stable blends.
implementation_hint: Add a second validation track: event-ordered forward splits by (Year,Race), with a small lap-level embargo around validation windows or whole-race holdouts for adjacent races. Use this track only for feature/model acceptance and ensemble selection, not necessarily for final fitting. Prefer candidates whose AUC degrades least from grouped CV to purged forward CV.
expected_effect: May not raise local CV, but should reduce overfitting to race-local temporal leakage and improve public-score transfer.
risk: The estimate will be noisier because there are only 26 races; if used as the only CV it may under-select useful models.
